amendments_note: spec 07 §1/§6.1: applies the degrade-to-uncapped amendment (ABC weights are properties of the shared posterior). The amendment rides N6; this artifact is its operational record under the NPV objective.

assumptions_manifest.note: spec 07 §9 N4 registry conversion (carried into N5): the constant-tier registry leaves capcost + this harness consume, each claiming a network-artifact row. The values_hash of these + the active prior bands enters the run_id preimage (D60 rec 3a).

candidate_universe.substitution_note: spec 07 §4.3: the candidate pool is meant to come from the stage-1 DRM screen re-run against the updated network (gate 1). Until spec 01 lands, the candidate universe is HAND-SUPPLIED -- the 13-arterial HANDOFF shortlist is the natural seed. This N1b run supplies the TWO corridors that already have committed configs + derived files [harbor, streetcar], which is exactly the point: 2 candidates -> 2 singles + 2 directional pairs + trivial swaps at full N=40,000 CRN, small enough to prove the sequencing machinery end to end. More candidates arrive with stage 1. This substitution is surfaced in the primary artifact (network_sequence.json.candidate_universe.hand_supplied).

archetype_gap.reason: archetypes are owner-designed complete networks with declared internal build orders (spec 07 §5.3 / §11 Q3); the harness must not generate them (a generator reintroduces the combinatorial problem this spec declines to solve). N3 supplies config/archetypes/*.json + the matched-capital gap series; until then the safeguard comparison line carries greedy vs best-single only.

LOW.delta_timing_note: continuation δ-timed at +1 cycle_gap on the profile 4% clock (spec 07 §3); own ΔNPV at cycle-0 timing (the common cycle-k deferral cancels in ranking, enters the frontier)

US_TYPICAL.delta_timing_note: continuation δ-timed at +1 cycle_gap on the profile 4% clock (spec 07 §3); own ΔNPV at cycle-0 timing (the common cycle-k deferral cancels in ranking, enters the frontier)

sigma_struct.note: std-based widening is the PRIMARY σ_struct measure (spec 07 N5 follow-up: an all-draws average, robust where the P90-P10 tail statistic can flip sign at finite n); P90-P10 kept as SECONDARY. Mean-zero noise leaves the P50 ~unchanged; the spread widens.

LOW.delta_timing_note: continuation δ-timed at +1 cycle_gap on the profile 4% clock (spec 07 §3); own ΔNPV at cycle-0 timing (the common cycle-k deferral cancels in ranking, enters the frontier)

US_TYPICAL.delta_timing_note: continuation δ-timed at +1 cycle_gap on the profile 4% clock (spec 07 §3); own ΔNPV at cycle-0 timing (the common cycle-k deferral cancels in ranking, enters the frontier)

sigma_struct.note: std-based widening is the PRIMARY σ_struct measure (spec 07 N5 follow-up: an all-draws average, robust where the P90-P10 tail statistic can flip sign at finite n); P90-P10 kept as SECONDARY. Mean-zero noise leaves the P50 ~unchanged; the spread widens.

interaction_matrix.tau_caveat: tau pinning (spec 07 §8a) pins transfer volume to a share of base boardings, so cross-line synergy in the objective is MUTED -- this interaction estimate is a lower bound on true complementarity; records-request item 3 is the eventual fix

frontier.aggregation: within-draw (spec 07 §3): per-draw ΔNPV summed inside each draw before percentiles; σ_struct adds per-line independent structural error (std-based widening PRIMARY, P90-P10 secondary -- N5 follow-up)
frontier.flyvbjerg_annotation: portfolio optimism > single-project optimism (correlated errors); moot here -- the portfolio is empty (spec 05 §4.3).
frontier.note: the §7 marginal stop fired before any commitment, so the recommended portfolio is EMPTY and the frontier is the cycle-0 CANDIDATE SCATTER: every candidate's standalone ΔNPV sits far below the ΔNPV=0 (BCR=1) line. This IS the build order in dollars -- build nothing at the welfare-BCA central profile.

sigma_struct.note: std-based widening is the PRIMARY σ_struct measure (spec 07 N5 follow-up: an all-draws average, robust where the P90-P10 tail statistic can flip sign at finite n); P90-P10 kept as SECONDARY. Mean-zero noise leaves the P50 ~unchanged; the spread widens.

std-based widening is the PRIMARY σ_struct measure (spec 07 N5 follow-up: an all-draws average, robust where the P90-P10 tail statistic can flip sign at finite n); P90-P10 kept as SECONDARY. Mean-zero noise leaves the P50 ~unchanged; the spread widens.

governance_note: in-run commitments are RECOMMENDATIONS (spec 07 §1); spec 00 §3 gate discipline applies at each REAL programmatic commitment. Here the recommendation is to BUILD NOTHING at the welfare-BCA central profile.

kernel_set.note: spec 07 §6.1: all candidates ranked under the SHARED OC posterior (543_launch_s500), uncapped alongside; ESS per ABC-weighted statistic.

objective.engine: tbc v3 wrapper bca-pipeline.mjs priced the in-memory run() export per candidate-given-network (spec 07 N5); per-draw ΔNPV read back for within-draw CV
objective.metric: ΔNPV (welfare-BCA), within-draw CV in common-base-year PV $M
objective.note: the NPV objective is the DEFAULT (spec 07 N5). The interim welfare-minutes objective is retained as --objective interim (the N4 regression anchor). CV per §3 with δ = one-cycle_gap deferral on the profile 4% clock; both cost bands carried; stopping rule §7 with the premium-bracket rows.

provenance_report.dependency_predicate: depends = omega>0.01 OR co-located persistent injection OR feeder/transfer edge in the rebuilt inputs.
provenance_report.depth_rule: depth(measured)=0; depth(eval)=1+max{depth(H): H hypothetical in network-before AND candidate depends on H}; committed line inherits eval depth; cap 2 -> EXPLORATORY, excluded from gate memos (spec 07 §6.2).
provenance_report.flyvbjerg: network optimism > single-project optimism (correlated errors); the depth cap is the control (spec 07 §6.2).
provenance_report.operating_mode: a planning tool re-run between real build cycles as lines open and forecasts become measurements; provenance depth resets from the updated network_asbuilt each re-run (spec 07 §6.2).
provenance_report.run_id_preimage: run_id = sha256 of canonical{candidates, asbuilt lines, seed, n, scenario, budget, assumptions_values_hash}. D60 review rec 3a added assumptions_values_hash = sha256{consumed capital constants + harness knobs + active prior bands}; it MOVES the id whenever the rate card or a prior band changes. Consequence recorded for provenance: the id here DIFFERS from the committed N1b/D60 artifact (d8b4a016...), which was keyed on inputs only and did not track the forecast-moving 60-mph v_cruise recenter. No timestamps enter the preimage (gate G6).

best_single_feasible.note: least-negative standalone ΔNPV; still below BCR=1

safeguard_comparison.greedy_portfolio_note: EMPTY -- the §7 marginal stop fires at cycle 1 (recommended portfolio is empty)
safeguard_comparison.note: safeguard = max{greedy portfolio, best single feasible, best feasible archetype}; here every option is below BCR=1, so the safeguard confirms the empty-portfolio verdict. Archetype leg N3.

sensitivity.base_note: the NPV stopping verdict (marginal BCR far below 1, recommended portfolio empty) is invariant to the heavy interim knobs (budget/omega/depth); those re-runs stay in --objective interim (spec 07 §10 G7).

landed_n5.note: spec 07 §6.1/§7 R2 premium-bracket rows on the marginal-BCR stop decision (first-order benefit-side scaling); LANDED at N5. Even the 2.0x row leaves the OC marginal BCR far below 1 -- the stop is robust.

spec 07 §3/§7: under a SLACK budget the CV rule orders by NPV LEVEL (the interchange argument), not by capital-efficiency ratio; the ratio becomes decision-relevant only when the budget binds. Moot here -- the stop fires at cycle 1 before any ordering matters.
both cost bands are carried on every candidate block, the frontier, and the stopping record (spec 04 §3.2 / spec 07 §7 'level-sensitive on both sides'). The stop fires on both bands.

named_spec_pending.note: cycle_gap moves the frontier's cumulative deferral and δ; moot at an empty portfolio (no committed step is deferred).
N5 follow-up: std-based widening is the PRIMARY σ_struct measure on every candidate block (P90-P10 secondary).

best_bcr_candidate.note: the highest marginal BCR any candidate reaches -- the closest OC comes to clearing BCR=1; still FAR below it.

stopping_record.economic_margin_note: the §7 marginal stop fires at cycle 0: the best candidate by NPV level ('streetcar') has CV <= 0 (marginal welfare BCR 0.042 US_TYPICAL / 0.060 LOW, ABC). The best BCR ANY OC ALM corridor reaches is 'harbor' at 0.092 (US_TYPICAL) / 0.135 (LOW) -- still FAR below the BCR=1 hurdle. No continuation is positive, so the decision-grade recommended portfolio is EMPTY: at the welfare-BCA central profile NO Orange County ALM corridor clears BCR=1. This is the ECONOMIC MARGIN at which the sequence stopped -- not 'candidates ran out' (spec 07 §7). The premium-bracket rows show the stop is robust to a 2x ASC premium.
stopping_record.marginal_candidate_note: 'streetcar' is the best candidate by NPV LEVEL (§3 slack-budget interchange rule: least-negative ΔNPV); its CV <= 0 fires the stop. Under a slack budget capital intensity is irrelevant to ORDER, so the level winner need NOT be the best-BCR line -- see best_bcr_candidate.
stopping_record.safeguard_note: pair-justified exemption did NOT apply (no continuation was positive); the §7 marginal stop is the binding verdict.
